title: Data Ownership, Integrity, Migrations, and Lifecycle Governance · Decision Evidence Lens: Data Reconciliation
summary: Shared records have unclear ownership, incompatible meanings, uncontrolled copies, irreversible migrations, and no correction or deletion path. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pp2 data reconciliation increment in governed data model and migration rehearsal. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair.
Implement retention, legal or policy hold, export, correction, deletion, derived-data cleanup, and auditable exceptions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:

security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments

steps.title: Predict Data Reconciliation · Theory Data Reconciliation
steps.instruction:
theory case — Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. The theory variation 3 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Data Reconciliation contract for the supplied data.

steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair. For Predict Data Reconciliation · Theory Data Reconciliation, keep Data Reconciliation connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Data Reconciliation while producing governed data model and migration rehearsal.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Three stores claim authority for the same entity while copied data, conflicting meanings, irreversible migration, and deletion gaps accumulate. Competency probe: Reconcile counts, checksums, invariants, samples, rejects, and actor-visible outcomes before and after migration or repair. A defensible solution applies Data Reconciliation, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to governed data model and migration rehearsal.

stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
